mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future earnings and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy rates are falling from an already restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not above baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS declines for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered sample hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a low-unemployment threshold signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months after UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or if the average return after the trigger is not statistically below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls while PAYEMS is flat or declining, the economy is shedding production and labor demand simultaneously, a contraction pattern that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling INDPRO and non-rising PAYEMS does not outperform the unconditional baseline in predicting negative 60-day returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal consumer spending and sales revenue, which should reduce earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 0 for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS growth episodes do not coincide with below-average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the mean return is positive across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real policy rates fall and the market interprets policy as behind the curve, which should hurt S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods where inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS do not produce lower 20-day forward returns than comparable non-triggered periods, or if the trigger effect disappears after controlling for recent market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST reflects tightening financial conditions and fading residential investment, a forward indicator of slower aggregate demand that should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if multi-month HOUST declines fail to precede below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the predictive sign flips positive in more than one major cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In periods where INDPRO is rising and PAYEMS is also rising, simultaneous improvement in industrial output and payroll growth signals broad real activity expansion, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint uptrend in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to improve 20-day forward returns relative to either series rising alone, or if the combined signal does not remain positive after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market deterioration regime marked by rising UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS implies weakening income growth and higher recession probability, which should turn S&P 500 5-day returns negative immediately after the deterioration becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the 5-day forward return after the trigger is not negative on average, or if triggered episodes do not show a materially lower hit rate than the unconditional sample.
